evals.prompt: Review this React authentication component (LoginForm.tsx). Is it production ready?
evals.expected_output: A structured review catching the seeded security and state defects: hardcoded live API key (CRITICAL), credentials logged via console.log, dangerouslySetInnerHTML on server-controlled error (XSS), token+user in localStorage with client-side isAdmin trust, useEffect with stale `attempts` closure and empty deps, setAttempts using stale state, no error handling on fetch/no rate limiting. Should give a score, Not Ready verdict, and concrete fixes.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: hardcoded live secret API key in client code (line 4) — public + must rotate
CRITICAL/HIGH: console.log of email AND password (line 25)
HIGH: dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders server error string — XSS sink (line ~59)
HIGH: auth token in localStorage (XSS-readable); client-side isAdmin trust for routing
HIGH: useEffect stale closure — reads `attempts` with empty deps, always 0; setTimeout never cleared
MEDIUM: setAttempts(attempts+1) stale state update (should be functional update)
MEDIUM: no error handling if fetch rejects; loading stuck; no login rate limiting
LOW: email input type='text' not 'email'; no validation
expectations: Output follows the mandated Code Review Summary structure with score, Critical Issues table, and Production Readiness verdict
Flags the hardcoded API key as CRITICAL
Flags console.log of the password as a sensitive-data leak
Flags dangerouslySetInnerHTML as an XSS sink
Identifies the useEffect stale-closure/empty-deps bug (state problem)
Verdict is Not Ready with reasoning tied to the findings
Does not fabricate issues that aren't in the code (no hallucinated findings)

evals.prompt: Perform a code review of this Node.js/Express orders API endpoint (orders.js). Find any bugs, security, and database problems.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: jwt.decode() instead of verify() — signature never checked, auth fully bypassable via forged token
CRITICAL: SQL injection via string-interpolated `status` and `req.user.id` into query (lines 23-27)
CRITICAL/HIGH: hardcoded JWT secret fallback 'dev-secret' (line 6)
HIGH: IDOR on GET /orders/:id — no user_id/ownership filter, any user reads any order (lines 54-62)
HIGH: N+1 query — order_items fetched per order in a loop (lines 33-41)
HIGH: cancel/refund does multiple writes with no transaction — partial failure leaves cancelled order without refund or vice versa
MEDIUM: fire-and-forget audit log db.query not awaited — errors vanish, unhandled rejection
MEDIUM: error response leaks err.message + err.stack to client
MEDIUM: parseInt(page) NaN when page missing → offset NaN
LOW: SELECT * ; no auth token presence check before decode
expectations: Output follows the mandated Code Review Summary structure
Flags jwt.decode-instead-of-verify as CRITICAL auth bypass
Flags the SQL injection in the status/query building as CRITICAL
Flags the IDOR / missing ownership check on GET /orders/:id
Flags the N+1 query pattern in the order loop
Flags the missing transaction around cancel + refund writes
Priority Fix Order lists CRITICAL items before lower severities
Does not hallucinate defects absent from the code

evals.prompt: Review this multi-tenant SaaS document service (documents_service.py). It's a FastAPI backend where users belong to organizations and documents belong to organizations. Check tenant isolation, authorization, and database security.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: list_documents has NO tenant/org filter AND SQL injection via `q` string concatenation — cross-tenant data exposure + injection
CRITICAL: get_document fetches by id with no org_id/tenant predicate — cross-tenant IDOR read
CRITICAL: delete_document deletes by id with no tenant/ownership/role check — any user deletes any org's docs
CRITICAL: create_document takes org_id from the request payload (client-supplied tenant) instead of the user's org — cross-tenant write / privilege issue
CRITICAL: /admin/all-documents returns ALL documents across ALL orgs with no admin role check and no tenant filter
HIGH: no RBAC/role enforcement anywhere; get_current_user only authenticates, never authorizes org membership
MEDIUM: share_document_cache key has no tenant prefix — cross-tenant cache leak surface
MEDIUM: SELECT * returns all columns; no response model (may leak internal fields)
expectations: Output follows the mandated Code Review Summary structure
Identifies missing tenant/org filtering on the document queries as CRITICAL cross-tenant leakage
Flags the client-supplied org_id in create_document as a tenant-boundary violation
Flags /admin/all-documents missing both role check and tenant scoping
Flags the SQL injection in list_documents
Addresses enforcement mechanism (RLS or scoped queries) not just per-endpoint patching
Notes the cache key tenant-leak surface OR response overexposure
Verdict is Not Ready; does not hallucinate absent defects

evals.prompt: Review this messy legacy pricing function (legacy_pricing.js). Detect code smells and suggest refactoring.
seeded_defects: HIGH (correctness): loop uses i <= items.length — off-by-one, reads undefined past the end, crashes on it.type
HIGH (correctness): coupon discount can exceed subtotal → negative price; no clamping; percent+fixed+gold stack unbounded
MEDIUM (correctness): float math on money throughout — rounding errors
MEDIUM: mutates input `user` (user.points +=, side effect in a calc function)
MEDIUM: God function — pricing, discount, tax, shipping, loyalty all in one; deep nesting
MEDIUM: magic numbers everywhere (0.1, 0.05, 0.2, tax rates, shipping thresholds)
MEDIUM: no input validation; opts.country unknown → tax 0 silently; coupon/user assumed shaped
LOW: var over const/let, == over ===, cryptic names (t, d, it, sub, pts), unused `data`/`items` duplication
LOW: `d = d + 0` dead branch
expectations: Output follows the mandated Code Review Summary structure
Catches the i <= items.length off-by-one as a real bug (not just a smell)
Flags the unbounded/negative discount possibility
Names concrete code smells (God function, magic numbers, deep nesting, poor naming)
Flags the input mutation side effect (user.points)
Provides before/after refactoring for at least one major issue with trade-offs
Distinguishes correctness bugs from style smells in severity (does not rate everything the same)
Suggestions are incremental, not an unjustified full rewrite
